volumeTitle: The Little Carthaginian. Pseudolus. The Rope
label: The Little Carthaginian (Section 5.4.1197b - Section 5.4.1243)

sections.label: Section 5.4.1197b
sections.translation: What ’ s the matter ?

sections.label: Section 5.4.1197c
sections.original: Ést lepida et lauta . út sapit .
sections.translation: She ’ s a clever and a nice girl . How shrewd she is !

sections.label: Section 5.4.1198
sections.translation: She has her father ’ s disposition in being shrewd .

sections.label: Section 5.4.1199
sections.original: Quae rés ? iam diu édepol sápientiám tuam haéc quidem abusast .
sections.translation: How ’ s that ? This long time , i ’ faith , she has surely used up your shrewdness .

sections.label: Section 5.4.1200
sections.original: nunc hínc sapit , hinc sentít , quidquid sapit , éx meo amore .
sections.translation: ’ Tis from here ( pointing to himself ) she now derives her shrewdness ; ’ tis from here her sense ; whatever she does shrewdly , through my love does she act so shrewdly .

sections.label: Section 5.4.1201
sections.original: Nón eo genere súmus prognatae , tam étsi sumus servaé , soror ,
sections.translation: We are not born of that rank , although we are slaves , sister , that it should befit us to do anything which any man may laugh at . Many are the faults of women ; but of the many , this one is the greatest , to please themselves too much , and to give their attention too little to pleasing the men .

sections.label: Section 5.4.1205
sections.original: Nimiae voluptatist quod in extis nostris portentumst , soror ,
sections.translation: It was a very great delight that was portended in our sacrifice of the entrails , sister , and what the soothsayer said about us both —

sections.label: Section 5.4.1206b
sections.translation: I wish he had said something about me !

sections.label: Section 5.4.1207
sections.original: Nos fore invíto domino nostro diebus paucis liberas .
sections.translation: That we should be free in a few days , in spite of our owner . I don ’ t know why I should hope for that , unless the Gods or our parents do something .

sections.label: Section 5.4.1209
sections.original: Mea fiducia hercle haruspex , patrue , his promisit , scio ,
sections.translation: ’ Twas through confidence in me [*] , kinsman , upon my faith , that the soothsayer promised them

sections.label: Section 5.4.1210
sections.original: libertatem , quia me amare hanc scit .
sections.translation: liberty , I ’ m sure of it , because he knows I ’ m in love with her .

sections.label: Section 5.4.1210b
sections.translation: Sister , follow me this way .

sections.label: Section 5.4.1210c
sections.translation: I follow .

sections.label: Section 5.4.1211
sections.original: Prius quam abitis , vos volo ambas . nisi piget , consistite .
sections.translation: Before you go away [*] , I want you both . Unless it ’ s inconvenient , stop .

sections.label: Section 5.4.1212
sections.translation: Who ’ s calling us back ?

sections.label: Section 5.4.1212b
sections.translation: One who wishes to do you a kindness .

sections.label: Section 5.4.1212c
sections.translation: There ’ s opportunity for doing it . But who is the person ?

sections.label: Section 5.4.1213b
sections.translation: A friend of yours .

sections.label: Section 5.4.1213c
sections.translation: One who is not an enemy , in fact .

sections.label: Section 5.4.1214
sections.original: Bonus est hic homo , mea voluptas .
sections.translation: This is a good man , my love .

sections.label: Section 5.4.1214b
sections.translation: I ’ faith , I should prefer him rather than a bad one .

sections.label: Section 5.4.1215
sections.original: Siquidem amicitiast habenda , cum hoc habendast .
sections.translation: If , indeed , friendship must be engaged in , with such a person ought it to be engaged in .

sections.label: Section 5.4.1215b
sections.translation: I don ’ t beg for it .

sections.label: Section 5.4.1216
sections.translation: He wishes to do you many services .

sections.label: Section 5.4.1216b
sections.translation: Being good yourself you will be doing good to the good .

sections.label: Section 5.4.1217
sections.translation: I will cause you joy —

sections.label: Section 5.4.1217b
sections.translation: And , i ’ faith , we pleasure to you .

sections.label: Section 5.4.1218
sections.translation: And liberty .

sections.label: Section 5.4.1218b
sections.original: Istoc pretio tuas nos facile feceris .
sections.translation: At that price you ’ ll easily make us your own .

sections.label: Section 5.4.1219
sections.original: Patrue mi , ita me di amabunt , ut ego , si sim Iuppiter ,
sections.translation: My kinsman , so may the Gods bless me , if I were Jupiter ,

sections.label: Section 5.4.1220
sections.original: iam hercle ego illam uxorem ducam et Iunonem extrudam foras .
sections.translation: upon my faith I ’ d at once marry her for my wife , and pack Juno out of doors . How quietly did she utter her words , how considerately and becomingly ! how modestly did she frame her speech ! certainly she is my own !

sections.label: Section 5.4.1222b
sections.translation: But how skilfully I accosted her !

sections.label: Section 5.4.1223b
sections.translation: Cleverly and becomingly , upon my faith .

sections.label: Section 5.4.1224
sections.translation: Am I still to go on testing them ?

sections.label: Section 5.4.1224b
sections.original: In pauca confer : sitiunt qui sedent .
sections.translation: Compress it in a few words ; the people who are sitting here are getting thirsty [*] .

sections.label: Section 5.4.1225
sections.original: Quid istic ? quod faciundumst cur non agimus ? in ius vos voco .
sections.translation: Well , why don ’ t we proceed to do that which was to be done ? ( To the WOMEN .) I summon you to justice .

sections.label: Section 5.4.1226
sections.original: Nunc , pátrue , tu frugí bonae es . tené . vin ego hanc adpréndam ?
sections.translation: Seize hold of this one , kinsman , if you are wise . Should you like me to catch hold of her ?

sections.label: Section 5.4.1227
sections.original: An patruos est , Agorastocles , tuos hic ?
sections.translation: Is this person your kinsman , Agorastocles ?

sections.label: Section 5.4.1227b
sections.translation: I ’ ll soon let you know . Now , by my word , I ’ ll be nicely revenged on you ; for I ’ ll make — you my bride .

sections.label: Section 5.4.1229
sections.original: Ite in ius , ne moramini . antestare me atque duce .
sections.translation: Come before a court of justice ; don ’ t delay !

sections.label: Section 5.4.1230
sections.original: Ego te antestabor , postea hanc amabo atque amplexabor .
sections.translation: Summon me as your witness [*] , and take me ; I ’ ll be a witness for you ; and after that , her ( pointing to ADELPHASIUM ) will I love and embrace . But ’ twas this , indeed , I intended to say — why yes , I did say that which I intended to say .

sections.label: Section 5.4.1232
sections.original: Moramini . in ius vos voco , nisi honestiust prehendi .
sections.translation: You are lingering . I summon you to justice , unless it is more becoming for you to be dragged thither .

sections.label: Section 5.4.1233
sections.original: Quid in iús vocas nos ? quid tibi debemus ?
sections.translation: Why do you summon us to justice ? What are we in your debt ?

sections.label: Section 5.4.1233b
sections.translation: He ’ ll tell it there .

sections.label: Section 5.4.1234
sections.translation: Are even my own dogs barking at me ?

sections.label: Section 5.4.1234b
sections.translation: Then , troth , do you caress me ;

sections.label: Section 5.4.1235
sections.original: dato mihi pro offa savium , pro ósse linguam obicito .
sections.translation: give me a kiss in place of a piece of meat ; present your lips in place of a bone [*] : that way I ’ ll render this dog more smooth for you than oil .

sections.label: Section 5.4.1237
sections.translation: Come on , if you are coming .

sections.label: Section 5.4.1237b
sections.translation: What have we done to you ?

sections.label: Section 5.4.1237c
sections.translation: You are thieves , both of you .

sections.label: Section 5.4.1238
sections.translation: What , we , as regards you ?

sections.label: Section 5.4.1238b
sections.translation: You , I say .

sections.label: Section 5.4.1238c
sections.translation: And I know it .

sections.label: Section 5.4.1238d
sections.translation: What theft is this ?

sections.label: Section 5.4.1238e
sections.translation: Enquire of him .

sections.label: Section 5.4.1239
sections.original: Quia annos multos filias meas célavistis clam me ,
sections.translation: Because for many years you have been concealing my daughters from me ,

sections.label: Section 5.4.1240
sections.original: atque equidem ingenuas liberas summoque genere gnatas .
sections.translation: and , in fact , persons free-born , and free , and born of the highest rank .

sections.label: Section 5.4.1241
sections.original: Numquam mecastor reperies tu istuc probrum penes nos .
sections.translation: I ’ faith , you ’ ll never find that villany to have been committed by us .

sections.label: Section 5.4.1242
sections.original: Da pignus , ni nunc perieres , in savium , uter utri det .
sections.translation: Make a bet of a kiss now , if you are not forsworn , which is to give it to the other .

sections.label: Section 5.4.1243
sections.translation: I ’ ve nothing to do with you ; prithee , get you gone .
